page: Amazon Web Services c7g.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom AWS Graviton3 processors. Built on the 64-bit ARM architecture (arm64) using Neoverse-V1 cores, this instance features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs with a single thread per core, running at a clock speed of 2.6 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio of 2.0 GB per core. The processor includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache, with hardware support for AES cryptography. No local storage is bundled with this instance, and network performance is rated at a baseline bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations position the c7g.xlarge in the average tier for most general compute and memory tasks. It achieves a stress-ng single-core score of 1532.58 and a multi-core score of 6111.85. Memory bandwidth is robust for cached reads at 385.54 GB/sec, though uncached large-block reads and writes drop to 106.7 GB/sec and 38.79 GB/sec respectively. The instance demonstrates average performance in software compilation with a Geekbench Clang score of 6152.0 and Redis SET operations at 6.65 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance in complex vector or floating-point tasks, such as Geekbench ray tracing and Passmark encryption, which is classified as poor. Large language model inference is also limited, with a 7B model text generation speed of 5.99 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM-based Graviton3 architecture provides a resource-dense profile that reduces power consumption and overhead compared to traditional x86 architectures, making it highly competitive for compute-bound workloads that do not require x86-specific instruction sets. Given its 1:2 memory-to-CPU ratio and lack of local storage or GPU accelerators, the c7g.xlarge is best suited for application servers, web serving, code compilation, and distributed data processing. It is also well-aligned with lightweight database operations and microservices that benefit from dedicated physical cores without hyperthreading.
description: Amazon Web Services c7g.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated physical cores powered by the 64-bit ARM-based AWS Graviton3 processor running at 2.6 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, and relies on a Nitro hypervisor. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, but offers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities for general compute, software compilation, and Redis operations, though it shows weaker results in cryptographic and complex floating-point tasks. Due to its dedicated ARM cores and high resource density, the c7g.xlarge delivers strong qualitative cost efficiency for compute-intensive workloads. It is highly suited for web serving, application servers, distributed microservices, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline, ideal for web serving and distributed microservices.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7g.xlarge is an ARM64 compute-optimized instance with 4 dedicated Graviton3 cores, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ARM-based, compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated physical cores and balanced memory bandwidth for efficient web serving and microservices.
bullet_points: Powered by 4 dedicated AWS Graviton3 ARM64 physical cores at 2.6 GHz
Configured with 8.0 GB of system memory and 32 MB of L3 cache
Delivers 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with no local storage
Achieves average performance in software compilation and Redis operations
Suited for web serving, application servers, and lightweight databases
